title: Shape Memory Alloy Production
entry_mechanism: PSAP Track 6 — KACST-international university SMA research programme transitioning to pilot production
executive_summary:
Shape Memory Alloys (SMAs) — primarily nickel-titanium (Nitinol) — are extraordinary materials that return to a pre-programmed shape when heated or when stress is removed. Applications span medical devices (coronary stents, orthodontic wires, surgical instruments), aerospace actuators, and advanced sensing systems. Saudi Arabia imports all Nitinol used in its expanding medical device and aerospace sectors.

Node AM-6 targets Saudi Arabia's entry into SMA production — specifically Nitinol for medical device applications (stents, guidewires) where Saudi Arabia's NIDLP medical device programme creates domestic demand, and for aerospace actuators where the SAMI programme creates a captive customer. Nitinol production requires vacuum induction melting and careful alloy composition control — technically demanding but achievable as a Phase II programme building on the materials science infrastructure from Phase I nodes.

The global SMA market was valued at $14.2 billion in 2024 growing at 12.4% CAGR, led by medical device applications. Saudi Arabia's Ti-rich mineral resource base and SABIC's nickel chemistry create potential feedstock advantages.

companies.name: Confluent Medical Technologies (Fort Wayne Metals)
companies.rationale: Confluent/Fort Wayne Metals is the world's leading Nitinol medical wire manufacturer with technology licensing capability.

companies.name: G. Rau GmbH
companies.rationale: G. Rau produces shape memory alloy products and has technology partnership programmes. Non-ITAR German technology.

companies.name: Memry Corporation (Admedes)
companies.rationale: Memry specialises in medical Nitinol and has a technology licensing track record.

companies.name: SAES Group
companies.rationale: SAES produces Nitinol and specialty alloys with international partnerships. Non-ITAR Italian technology.

companies.name: ATI (Allegheny Technologies)
companies.rationale: ATI produces Nitinol and specialty titanium alloys with technology licensing capability.

partners.name: KACST Materials Science
partners.current: Alloy research; some shape memory research; materials characterisation
partners.upgrade: GADD-KACST Nitinol research programme; vacuum induction melting facility; SMA characterisation and testing; G. Rau or SAES technology partnership

partners.name: KFUPM Materials / Metallurgy
partners.current: Metallurgy; alloy processing; some SMA theory
partners.upgrade: SMA engineering specialisation; KACST-KFUPM Nitinol research programme; Saudi SMA engineers pipeline

partners.name: Ma'aden Titanium (future)
partners.current: Aluminium and phosphate mining; some titanium mineral assessment
partners.upgrade: Saudi titanium as feedstock for Nitinol production; Ma'aden titanium sponge programme as Phase II enabler for Saudi SMA

partners.name: Saudi Medical Device Companies (NIDLP)

partners.upgrade: Saudi SMA wire as input for stent and guidewire manufacturing; NIDLP anchor demand for Phase II SMA production scale-up
